title: Stateless vs Stateful Workers
concept: Handling session state across stateless API endpoints and stateful background task runners.

exercises.prompt: What is a key benefit of using background worker nodes at scale?

options.text: They can create more locks in the database.
They help manage multiple concurrent tasks without conflicts.
They make the server slow down overall.

exercises.explain: Background worker nodes allow us to manage multiple tasks at once without getting stuck due to conflicts.

cards.title: Stateless vs Stateful Workers
cards.body: In programming, workers can be either stateless, meaning they don't remember past actions, or stateful, which means they can remember information throughout a session.

cards.title: Stateless Workers
cards.body: Stateless workers do not maintain any session information. They treat each request as a new one, like a vending machine that doesn't remember past selections.

cards.title: Stateful Workers
cards.body: A stateful worker is like a personal assistant who remembers your preferences and past conversations, so they can provide a personalized experience each time.

cards.title: Stateless Example
cards.body: An API that provides weather data is stateless. Each time you ask, it doesn’t remember your previous requests; it simply provides the current weather.

cards.title: Myth: Stateless is Always Better
cards.body: Some believe stateless systems are superior, but stateful workers are crucial for tasks needing context, like shopping carts or user sessions.

cards.title: Comparison of Stateless vs Stateful
cards.body: Understanding how these workers differ helps in choosing the right one for your application.

left.text: Does not remember past data, each request is new.

right.text: Remembers past data, allowing for continuous sessions.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following describes a stateless worker?

options.text: Processes each request individually without memory.
Stores user data after each task is completed.

exercises.explain: A stateless worker does not remember any past requests, treating each one independently.

exercises.prompt: Stateful workers do not retain information between tasks.
exercises.explain: Stateful workers do retain information between tasks, which allows them to provide context and continuity.

exercises.prompt: An API providing current data like weather is a __________ worker.
exercises.sentence: An API providing current data like weather is a stateless worker.
exercises.explain: The correct answer is 'stateless' because it does not retain any information between requests.

exercises.prompt: Which example illustrates a stateful worker?

options.text: A chat application remembering user messages.
A calculator performing operations afresh each time.
A public library providing books to anyone.

exercises.explain: The chat application remembers previous messages and context, making it stateful.

exercises.prompt: Which scenario is best suited for a stateless worker?
exercises.explain: Fetching the latest stock prices is a stateless operation; each request for prices does not depend on any previous requests.
